root_causes: Both deletion cohorts collapsed to one rare true class rather than spanning the task decision surface.
Unique cohort markers appeared at the final token position consumed by the frozen representation, making marker shift dominate semantic attributes.
The sacrificial preflight tested overall classification but did not require a deletion-aware retraining comparator to solve deletion-like held-out cases.
Consequently, behavioral changes, membership scores, and distance-to-retraining metrics were measurable but not an informative test of unlearning success.

changes: balance all three true classes within both deletion cohorts and matched nonmembers
move identifiers away from the terminal representation position and use attention-masked mean pooling
add a sacrificial deletion-like comparator adequacy floor before opening held-out outcomes
score membership attack advantage separately from raw AUC direction
preserve the v1 corpus, code identities, raw artifacts, result, and failed interpretation unchanged

non_claims: The v1 integrity validator passing does not make the unlearning target informative.
The v1 run is not a claim result and cannot satisfy the M7 full-attempt gate.
No language-model unlearning, influence reduction, privacy improvement, erasure, transfer, or support promotion follows.
